summary_one_line: IHDR color_type byte (offset 0x19) must equal 3 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE), not the common value 6 (RGBA).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at file offset 0x19 must hold the exact value 0x03 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) so that png_ptr->color_type compares equal at pngrutil.c:1973; the loser inputs instead carry 0x06 (RGBA), which fails the equality and routes to the gray/color (false-side) arms.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds carry 03 at offset 0x19 (W bytes 03(.)x3) and take the true branch at L1973 (W T=3 F=0), reaching the palette-index body at lines 1974-1983.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 06 at offset 0x19 (L bytes 06(.)x19) and never take the true side at L1973 (L T=0 F=19), instead falling through to the COLOR_MASK_COLOR else arm at L1996/L2014.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both arms have cmplog/I2S, so I2S is held constant and cannot be the differentiator; the decisive delta is value_profile. value_profile adds the CMP_MAP gradient: the equality png_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE is intercepted, and the Hamming/prefix-distance bucket rewards inputs whose color_type byte moves toward the constant 3, letting value_profile_cmplog discover the rarer color_type=3 PNG that plain cmplog could not.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an exact equality of color_type against PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE at pngrutil.c:1973, with the true side globally blocked.
Winner seeds hold byte 0x03 at offset 0x19 while loser seeds hold 0x06 there.
Winner inputs take the true side of the blocker branch; loser inputs take only the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1973  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=19  if (png_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Losers route to the COLOR_MASK_COLOR arm instead of the palette body.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1996  T=0 F=0  T=0 F=19  else if ((png_ptr->color_type & PNG_COLOR_MASK_COLOR) == ...

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over cmplog with the differing technique being value_profile.
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of the CMP buffer.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so lineage verification is not mandatory. I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3999 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2dbd8ff81fa7c2ca` and observed an I2S-floor dash row at depth 7. That confirms I2S participated, but since the loser (cmplog) also has I2S, I2S is held constant across the pair and cannot be the differentiator; the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient is the decisive technique, so I do not assert I2S as the verified winning mechanism.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on color_type == 3 where plain cmplog resolves the branch as fast as value_profile_cmplog (no gradient advantage), showing the single-byte equality is solvable by I2S substitution alone without value_profile.

weakest_evidence_point: The CMP_MAP gradient attribution rests on the value_profile delta label plus an equality CMP shape; the lineage shows an I2S dash row that could mean I2S already solved this byte in both arms, weakening the claim that value_profile is uniquely responsible.
